mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during recession onset.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60 percent of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated inflation indicates severe supply chain constraints and margin compression, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO falls during high inflation, or the mechanism fails to predict direction in over 50 percent of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 3.5 percent forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 3.5, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly expands the labor force and consumer base, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of observations, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and accelerating retail sales signals unsustainable consumption financed by inventory drawdowns, leading to negative near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence pattern produces positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during active monetary tightening demonstrates resilient supply chains absorbing higher financing costs, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to materialize when INDPRO rises during tightening periods, or the joint signal loses predictive accuracy in more than 60 percent of tightening months.
